agents.description: Use when: changing sattlint CLI commands, argparse routing, interactive app menus, config flows, tools menu behavior, or console UX in src/sattlint/app.py
agents.name: CLI App Menu

agents.description: Use when: changing DOCX generation, documentation classification, FS grouping, unit-scope selection, or docgen CLI workflow in SattLint
agents.name: Documentation Generation

agents.description: Use when: changing SattLine grammar, parser, transformer, AST, strict validation, analyzers, syntax-check behavior, or semantic rules in SattLint
agents.name: Parser Analysis

agents.description: Use when: scoping a broad request, choosing the owning SattLint surface, planning file claims and handoffs, or deciding whether work should stay in one slice or split across multiple specialist agents
agents.name: Planner

agents.description: Use when: changing repo audit checks, pipeline artifacts, public-readiness scans, portability checks, secrets and PII scanning, or devtools audit commands in SattLint
agents.name: Repo Audit

agents.description: Use when: running precommit checks, audit checks, verifying the repo is clean, checking before committing, running verify script, repo verification, pre-push checks, lint and test gate
agents.name: Repo Verify

agents.description: Use when: reviewing an executor or test handoff, checking architectural compliance, confirming finish-gate evidence, or deciding whether a SattLint slice is ready for merge
agents.name: Reviewer Agent

agents.description: Use when: splitting work across multiple chats or agents, parallelizing repo tasks, coordinating file ownership, planning handoffs, or orchestrating parser, LSP, and verification work in SattLint
agents.name: SattLint Orchestrator

agents.description: Use when: validating an executor slice from a handoff, adding focused regression or edge-case tests, rerunning owner-suite proof, or updating handoff validation state in SattLint
agents.name: Test Agent

agents.description: Use when: changing workspace loading, editor_api, semantic core, language server, dependency resolution, dirty-buffer parsing, or VS Code client behavior in SattLint
agents.name: Workspace LSP

blocking_invariant_rules.details: Do not widen scope before that check when a narrow executable validation exists.
blocking_invariant_rules.summary: Run the first focused executable validation immediately after the first substantive edit.

blocking_invariant_rules.details: Targeted pytest or repo-local tool commands are the expected first validation path in this repo.
blocking_invariant_rules.summary: Use repo venv commands, not the VS Code test runner, for first validation.

blocking_invariant_rules.details: Python finish gates require focused behavior validation plus touched-file Ruff and Pyright checks.
blocking_invariant_rules.summary: Do not finish Python edits with touched-file Ruff or Pyright errors.

blocking_invariant_rules.details: Use owner-suite validation or quick repo audit after the narrow check for shared infra or cross-subsystem wiring.
blocking_invariant_rules.summary: Shared infra and devtools changes widen only after the focused check passes.

blocking_invariant_rules.details: CLI menu invariants require the interactive app tests to move with the menu surface.
blocking_invariant_rules.summary: If CLI menu layout or numbering changes, update tests/test_app.py in the same change.

blocking_invariant_rules.details: Run sattlineLsp.restartServer after changes under the editor or workspace loading surfaces.
blocking_invariant_rules.summary: Restart the language server after semantic core, LSP, editor_api, or VS Code client edits.

default_entrypoint.description: Default machine entrypoint for agents. Returns changed files, owning surface, instruction files, first focused validation, finish-gate plan, and blocking invariants in one response.

finish_gate_templates.description: Shared-pipeline finish gate for changes that do not need repo-audit-specific checks.
Combined repo-audit finish gate for changes that require repo-audit-specific checks.

instructions.description: Use when changing AGENTS, custom agents, prompts, skills, hooks, or other repo AI customizations in SattLint. Covers discoverability, context efficiency, and hook safety rules.
instructions.name: Agent Customization Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing analyzer registry wiring, analyzer entry points, helper ownership, or analyzer architecture tests in SattLint.
instructions.name: Analyzer Architecture

instructions.description: Use when changing sattlint CLI routing, argparse surfaces, interactive app menus, config flows, or console UX in SattLint. Covers menu and command invariants plus test routing.
instructions.name: CLI App Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing DOCX generation, documentation classification, FS grouping, unit scope selection, or app documentation workflows in SattLint. Covers docgen-specific boundaries and tests.
instructions.name: Documentation Generation Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing parser-core, strict validation, grammar, AST, transformer, parser fixtures, or parser tests in SattLint. Covers parser invariants and first validation routing.
instructions.name: Parser Analysis Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing Python tests in SattLint. Covers focused pytest routing, fixture reuse, and stable assertion patterns for repo-venv workflows.
instructions.name: Python Test Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing ratchet baselines, debt ledgers, approval records, coverage floors, or ratchet-policy tests in SattLint. Covers approval, monotonicity, per-file debt, and focused validation rules.
instructions.name: Ratchet Policy Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing repo audit checks, pipeline artifacts, public-readiness scans, portability checks, or devtools JSON outputs in SattLint. Covers preferred seams and focused validation.
instructions.name: Repo Audit Instructions

instructions.description: Use when routing work to the owning SattLint surface or choosing the closest repo area for a change. Provides the condensed repo map that was removed from AGENTS.md for context efficiency.
instructions.name: Repo Map Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing src/ or tests/ in SattLint. Critical SattLine language invariants, validation rules, variable-analysis gotchas, CLI testing rules, and workspace/LSP boundaries that must not be broken.
instructions.name: SattLine Invariants

instructions.description: Use when changing SattLint test fixtures, corpus manifests, sample SattLine files, or golden analysis artifacts. Covers fixture shape, corpus layout, and minimal SattLine validity rules.
instructions.name: Test Fixture Instructions

instructions.description: Use when changing workspace loading, semantic core, editor facade, dirty-buffer parsing, LSP diagnostics, or VS Code client behavior in SattLint. Covers workspace-specific invariants and restart rules.
instructions.name: Workspace LSP Instructions

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use after touched Python edits for fast style, import-order, and lint hygiene proof.
pipeline_checks.label: Run Ruff

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use when touched Python files need static type proof before widening to broader finish gates.
pipeline_checks.label: Run pyright

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use targeted owner pytest first for behavior proof on Python changes.
pipeline_checks.label: Run pytest

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use when dead-code proof is relevant for Python infra or audit-facing changes.
pipeline_checks.label: Run Vulture

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use when security-sensitive Python edits need static security scan proof.
pipeline_checks.label: Run Bandit

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use when architecture, dependency, or structural-budget artifacts need regeneration.
pipeline_checks.label: Collect structural reports

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use when parser, analyzer, or workspace-loading edits need trace or profiling artifacts.
pipeline_checks.label: Collect trace report

pipeline_checks.ai_summary: Use when parser or analyzer changes need corpus-level regression proof.
pipeline_checks.label: Run corpus suite

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when documentation or Python sources may have leaked local paths, secrets, or unsafe text.
repo_audit_checks.label: Scan repository text for leaks and local paths

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when changes may rely on local-only paths, guards, or machine-specific assumptions.
repo_audit_checks.label: Detect local-versus-CI parity drift in paths, test guards, and local dependency roots

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when CLI help, command docs, or agent reference commands must stay in sync with implementation.
repo_audit_checks.label: Check documented commands against implemented CLI surfaces

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when config declarations or config consumers change and unused keys may drift.
repo_audit_checks.label: Report declared but unused config keys

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when Python architecture, import layering, or module-size constraints may shift.
repo_audit_checks.label: Run repository architecture checks

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when structural budget artifacts or their translation into findings may change.
repo_audit_checks.label: Translate structural report findings into repo-audit findings

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when CLI parser descriptions, subcommand help, or interactive command surfaces change.
repo_audit_checks.label: Validate CLI descriptions and subcommand help

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when library code changes may introduce unexpected prints or weak failure-path diagnostics.
repo_audit_checks.label: Check library modules for unexpected print calls

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when AI-generated artifacts, coordination state, or related cleanup policy changes.
repo_audit_checks.label: Report stale AI-generated artifacts and oversized local coordination state

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when repo-local ignored paths or hidden dependency roots may leak into tracked code.
repo_audit_checks.label: Detect ignored repo-local dependency references

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when AI instructions, agents, generated routing maps, or other AI-control surfaces change.
repo_audit_checks.label: Enforce AI harness freshness for instructions, agents, links, and generated maps

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when coverage artifacts or audit-facing coverage recommendations may change.
repo_audit_checks.label: Translate low-coverage modules into audit findings

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when top-level repo hygiene, public metadata, or publish-facing docs may drift.
repo_audit_checks.label: Check public-repository readiness files and metadata

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when ratchet-policy wiring, debt ledgers, or full-audit change-proof enforcement changes.
repo_audit_checks.label: Run ratchet policy for change-scoped debt and size enforcement

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when routing catalogs, recommendation metadata, or generated AI registry outputs change.
repo_audit_checks.label: Verify recommendation metadata and routing catalog coverage

repo_audit_checks.ai_summary: Use when CLI consistency reporting or command-reference alignment changes.
repo_audit_checks.label: Build the full CLI consistency report
